rationale.R1.1: The gap statement, contribution claim, and framing of TransitDRR versus aggregate metrics are all present but unevenly developed; sharpening them is standard scope for 'improve this draft' on a scientific abstract.
rationale.R1.2: The draft contains at least three internal consistency problems: (a) paragraph 4 asserts independence of the three axes to justify their product as a joint probability, but independence is nowhere established; (b) paragraph 4 states the per-axis breakdown is omitted for brevity, yet the final paragraph reports a per-axis finding (M-dwarf variability drop); (c) the recommended threshold of ≥0.80 is set above the observed maximum of 0.71, implying no pipeline currently qualifies. These are load-bearing if technical review is in scope, peripheral if only prose polish is requested.
rationale.R1.5: The contradiction between §4's claim to collapse to a scalar and the final paragraph's per-axis result is also a structural problem: the abstract's logical arc breaks at that seam and may require re-ordering or splitting those paragraphs.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'improve this draft' canonically centers on prose quality; R1.6 is load-bearing regardless of how the technical-review ambiguity resolves.
